schema.description: Clinical intake + evaluation form for a physiotherapy episode. Field structure transcribed from the current Fisiozero physiotherapy episode form (NOVO EPISODIO, FISIOTERAPIA). PT is the source language; EN labels are provided for the bilingual UI. ai_extractable is set to false on every field pending the AI-partner contract; flip to true per-field once the ingestion contract is signed off.

linked_appointment.x-note: References an existing appointment; resolved by the app, not free text.

cid_codes.x-note: ICD/CID-10 codes associated to the episode.

x-hint.pt: Clique no bodychart e seleccione o marcador pretendido. Pode inserir tantos pontos quantos desejar.
x-hint.en: Click on the bodychart and select the desired marker. Insert as many points as needed.

bodychart.description: Array of markers placed on the body diagram. Identical marker set to the osteopathy template. Each marker has a type and coordinates.

x.x-note: Normalized X coordinate on the bodychart (0–1).

y.x-note: Normalized Y coordinate on the bodychart (0–1).

view.x-note: Which of the 4 body views the marker sits on.

x-label.pt: Notas Pessoais sobre o Utente neste Episódio / Consulta
x-label.en: Private Notes about the Patient in this Episode / Consultation

x-hint.pt: Este campo não é partilhado com nenhum outro utilizador da sua conta.
x-hint.en: This field is not shared with any other user on your account.

private_notes.x-note: Access-controlled: visible only to the practitioner who authored it. Enforcement is an app/RLS concern, not a schema concern — flagged for lead. Must NOT be exposed to the AI ingestion pipeline regardless of how ai_extractable evolves.

x-open-questions: private_notes is marked 'not shared with any other user' in the live form. This needs row-level-security / access-control enforcement at the db + app layer (lead-owned). It is hard-set ai_extractable:false and should stay that way — private clinical notes must never be fed to the AI extraction pipeline. Confirm the lead models this as a restricted column / separate table with RLS.

Bodychart coordinate model: same open question as osteopathy — confirm normalized (0–1) + view enum vs pixel coords. Kept identical to osteopathy template for consistency; both should resolve the same way.

Field overlap with osteopathy: episode_date, weight, height, linked_appointment, cid_codes, red_flags, bodychart, treatment_objectives/plan, observations are shared between both templates. Worth the lead deciding whether these live in a shared base schema that both templates extend, vs duplicated per template. Modelled as duplicated for now since the two forms diverge in the middle (physio: queixas/antecedentes/medicação/diagnóstico; osteopathy: problemas de saúde grid + anamnese por sistemas).
Required fields: only episode_date + main_complaints marked required, matching the '•' markers on the live form. Confirm.
Are weight_kg / height_cm relevant to physio episodes, or vestigial from a shared form? Live form shows them; modelled as optional.
